description: Contract-backed smoke proof for topology-aware blocking-pool affinity saturation, locality accounting, and safe fallback behavior.

smoke_scenarios.description: Compare disabled and cohort-biased blocking-pool affinity under deterministic saturation and freeze the operator-visible locality/fallback report.

notes: Execute mode is deterministic because the proof uses fixed cohort count, fixed queue distribution, and barrier-gated blocking tasks.
The workload is small enough for normal scoped rch validation; it does not require a real 64-core host to prove the accounting surface.

template_env.BLOCKING_POOL_AFFINITY_SMOKE_ARTIFACT_RO: Override the retrieved affinity-report output root.

recommended_for: 64+ core hosts that mix async coordination with bursts of blocking parsing, decompression, or helper work
operators who want explicit cohort-local queueing and spill accounting before turning on larger host profiles
avoid_when: topology is absent or misleading and the disabled baseline remains easier to reason about
blocking helpers are too small or too sparse to benefit from cohort-local queue bias
operator_notes.no_win_trigger: pin the disabled profile if cohort-biased locality stops winning or if its p95 completion latency exceeds the disabled profile by 4x

capture_plan: Run the runtime_blocking_pool_queue_when_saturated_audit integration test with the contract path, scenario id, and report output path injected through the environment.
Extract the blocking-pool affinity report JSON from the test log, persist it beside the run manifest, and validate its stable report_projection fields against this contract.

smoke_scenarios.description: Drive blocking helpers from async coordinators through a live runtime handle and freeze the mixed-workload locality, queue-depth, and spill report.

notes: Execute mode builds a real runtime with 2 async workers and a 2-thread blocking pool, then uses async coordinators to enqueue cohort-targeted blocking helpers.
The workload is deterministic because the worker/cohort map, async coordinator count, blocking spawn count, and release barriers are all fixed by the scenario contract.

template_env.BLOCKING_POOL_AFFINITY_SMOKE_ARTIFACT_RO: Override the retrieved affinity-report output root.

recommended_for: 64+ core hosts where async coordinators fan out bursts of CPU-heavy or blocking helper work
operators who want an end-to-end proof that cohort-biased blocking helpers improve locality without stranding mixed workloads
avoid_when: blocking helpers are extremely sparse and the disabled profile is already sufficient
host topology hints are unavailable and only the conservative disabled path is desired
operator_notes.no_win_trigger: pin the disabled profile if the mixed async-plus-blocking report stops showing lower global queue spill or if cohort-biased p95 completion latency exceeds the disabled profile by 4x

capture_plan: Build a runtime with 2 async workers, a 2-thread blocking pool, and an explicit 2-cohort worker map.

Use async coordinators to enqueue cohort-targeted blocking helpers while both blocking workers are held behind release barriers, then snapshot pending queue depths, local-vs-remote execution, spill counts, and fallback activations before release.
Extract the blocking-pool affinity report JSON from the test log, persist it beside the run manifest, and validate its stable report_projection fields against this contract.

smoke_scenarios.description: Drive unhinted blocking helpers from async coordinators and prove that topology-aware affinity safely falls back to the disabled profile when no cohort-locality win exists.

notes: Execute mode builds a real runtime with 2 async workers and a 2-thread blocking pool, but the async coordinators enqueue unhinted blocking helpers through the global path.

The workload is deterministic because the worker/cohort map, async coordinator count, blocking spawn count, and release barriers are fixed, while the lack of cohort hints forces both profiles onto the same safe fallback path.

template_env.BLOCKING_POOL_AFFINITY_SMOKE_ARTIFACT_RO: Override the retrieved affinity-report output root.

recommended_for: operators validating that topology-aware affinity can safely stand down when blocking helpers arrive without cohort hints
shared 64+ core hosts where some producers are topology-blind and the runtime must prove it will not fabricate locality wins
avoid_when: the workload already carries reliable cohort hints and should be measured with the targeted mixed scenario instead
you need the aggressive locality-biased path even when no worker/cohort metadata is available
operator_notes.no_win_trigger: keep the disabled profile pinned whenever the unhinted async-plus-blocking replay shows identical queue pressure and zero locality wins across both profiles

capture_plan: Build a runtime with 2 async workers, a 2-thread blocking pool, and an explicit 2-cohort worker map.

Use async coordinators to enqueue unhinted blocking helpers while both blocking workers are held behind release barriers, then snapshot global queue pressure, per-cohort queue depth, and zero-locality execution counts before release.
Extract the blocking-pool affinity report JSON from the test log, persist it beside the run manifest, and validate its stable report_projection fields against this contract.
